args:
set -eu
PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
PROJECT_DIR=$(pwd)
PROJECT_SETTINGS="$PROJECT_DIR/.codanna/settings.toml"
PROJECT_INDEX_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/.codanna/index"

if ! command -v codanna >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Codanna is not installed. Run the 'codanna-install' skill to install the global codanna CLI for this machine." >&2
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$PROJECT_SETTINGS" ] || [ ! -d "$PROJECT_INDEX_DIR" ] || ! find "$PROJECT_INDEX_DIR" -mindepth 1 -print -quit 2>/dev/null | grep -q .; then
  echo "Codanna is not initialized for this project. Run the 'codanna-init' skill in $PROJECT_DIR to create .codanna and build the first index." >&2
  exit 1
fi

codanna index . >/dev/null
INDEX_INFO=$(codanna mcp get_index_info 2>/dev/null || true)
if printf '%s\n' "$INDEX_INFO" | grep -Eq 'Symbols:[[:space:]]*0|Files:[[:space:]]*0|Embeddings:[[:space:]]*0'; then
  echo "Codanna index exists but semantic search is not ready yet. Run the 'codanna-init' skill and check 'codanna mcp get_index_info' before relying on semantic search." >&2
  exit 1
fi

exec codanna serve --watch
codanna.note: Apple Silicon macOS stdio launcher. Finds global `codanna`, verifies the project index, refreshes it, then runs `codanna serve --watch`. Use `codanna-install` or `codanna-init` if setup is missing.
